mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that immediately suppress equity demand and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 8.0%, or signal Rank IC falls below 0.03 in post-2009 data.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with stable inflation indicates robust consumer purchasing power, which drives corporate earnings growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which generates short-term momentum in equity markets during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5% quarterly.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity, which consistently precedes positive broad market returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts show zero correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows since 2000.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly inflation spikes trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >50% of months following a 0.5% CPI jump, or the signal loses significance when FEDFUNDS is already above 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, which reliably supports positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when GDPC1 contracts, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP contractions signal economic recession, which immediately reduces corporate profit expectations and generates negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: GDP contractions fail to predict negative 60-day returns, or the signal produces positive returns in >40% of contraction quarters due to preemptive monetary easing.
